name: Marche (Italy)
description: A white field charged at the centre with a stylised green woodpecker (Picus), the regional emblem of the Marche.
history: Adopted by Regional Law n. 8 of 15 February 1980, the flag features the stylised Picus designed by Michele Provinciali. The woodpecker recalls the legend of the ver sacrum, the sacred springtime migration in which the Sabine Picentes were said to have been led south by a Picus (woodpecker) into the territory of ancient Picenum.

features.symbolism: A white field charged with the stylised regional emblem, a green Picus (woodpecker), a totemic animal of the ancient Italic peoples of the area.

In Roman tradition the Picus (woodpecker) led the ver sacrum migration of the Sabine Picentes south into what is now the Marche, giving its name to the Picentes and the territory of Picenum. The stylised green Picus has been the regional emblem since 1980.
